 {"id":"bd-2zqr","title":"Webhook notification on install completion","description":"## Overview
Add optional webhook notification when installation completes, useful for fleet management and monitoring.

## Use Cases
- DevOps teams deploying to multiple VPS
- Automated provisioning pipelines
- Personal notifications (Slack, Discord)
- Monitoring dashboards

## Configuration
```bash
# Via environment variable
export ACFS_WEBHOOK_URL="https://hooks.slack.com/services/..."
acfs install

# Via flag
acfs install --webhook "https://example.com/hook"

# Via config file
echo 'webhook_url: "https://..."' >> ~/.config/acfs/config.yaml
```

## Webhook Payload
```json
{
  "event": "install_complete",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-25T23:00:00Z",
  "hostname": "my-vps",
  "ip": "1.2.3.4",
  "duration_seconds": 180,
  "tools_installed": 26,
  "tools_failed": 0,
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "errors": []
}
```

## Implementation Details
1. Check for webhook config at install start
2. Collect metrics during install
3. POST JSON payload on completion
4. Non-blocking (don't fail install if webhook fails)
5. Timeout: 5 seconds

## Security Considerations
- HTTPS only (reject HTTP webhooks)
- Don't send sensitive data (no paths, no env vars)
- User must explicitly configure (opt-in)
- Log webhook failures but don't expose URL

## Slack/Discord Formatting
```bash
# Auto-detect common webhook formats
if [[ "$url" == *"hooks.slack.com"* ]]; then
  format_slack_message
elif [[ "$url" == *"discord.com/api/webhooks"* ]]; then
  format_discord_message
fi
```

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test webhook fires on completion
- [ ] Test payload contains correct data
- [ ] Test webhook failure doesn't break install
- [ ] Test HTTPS-only enforcement
- [ ] Test Slack/Discord formatting

## Files to Modify
- scripts/lib/webhook.sh (new)
- scripts/install.sh (add webhook call)
- scripts/lib/config.sh (read webhook config)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:02:39.201612806Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:10:55.186533565Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zqr","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:04:50.840152260Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-31ps","title":"EPIC: ACFS no-brainer improvements (manifest sync + support + UX)","description":"## Goal
Deliver the 10 most obviously accretive improvements identified via idea-wizard: manifest-driven web content, support bundle, structured install logging, wizard command builder, manifest-driven doctor checks + fix hints, doctor --fix, preflight network health, pinned-ref installer UX, module failure resume hints, and a web tool-status page.

## Why
These are low-risk, high-impact improvements that reduce drift, improve debuggability, and make the beginner UX more deterministic. They align with ACFS’s single-source-of-truth philosophy and reduce support burden.

## Scope
All changes remain within existing architecture: manifest + generator, install.sh orchestration, scripts/lib, and apps/web. No new backends.

## Non-Goals
- No re-architecture of installer phases
- No new external services
- No breaking changes without flags

## High-Level Deliverables
1. Manifest to web generated data (tools/tldr/commands/lessons index).
2. acfs support-bundle command (logs + state + doctor JSON + versions).
3. Structured install logs + JSON summary artifacts.
4. Wizard command builder with shareable URL state.
5. Doctor uses generated checks + per-module fix suggestions.
6. acfs doctor --fix for safe deterministic fixes.
7. Preflight network + repo health checks.
8. Pinned ref UX (--pin-ref + wizard toggle).
9. Auto-resume hint on module failure.
10. Web tool status page generated from manifest.

## Success Criteria
- Docs and generator stay in sync.
- Support/debugging becomes one-command reproducible.
- Wizard reduces copy/paste errors.
- acfs doctor provides actionable fixes and optional auto-fix.
- All changes have unit tests and e2e scripts with detailed logging.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:19:03.423708833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:19:03.423708833Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-31ps.1","title":"Manifest-driven web content generation (tools/tldr/commands/lessons)","description":"## Background
Website tool lists (flywheel.ts, tldr-content.ts, commands.ts, lessons index) are hand-maintained and drift from acfs.manifest.yaml. ACFS’s single-source-of-truth goal implies the manifest should drive web content to eliminate duplication.

## Goal
Generate web-facing tool/lesson data from the manifest so the website always reflects the real install set and metadata.

## Scope
- Extend manifest schema/types with web metadata fields (display_name, short_desc, category, docs_url, lesson_slug, tldr_snippet, command_examples, etc.).
- Update generator to emit apps/web/lib/generated/*.ts.
- Refactor web app to consume generated data.

## Considerations
- Must keep backward compatibility with existing content; provide migration adapters.
- Generated files live under apps/web/lib/generated and are NEVER edited manually.
- Keep web bundle size stable (tree-shake generated modules).

## Testing
- Generator unit tests for deterministic output.
- Web type-check + build.
- Web E2E smoke for pages that use generated data.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No manual edits needed in flywheel.ts/tldr-content.ts/commands.ts/lessons.ts for tool lists.
- A single manifest change updates web content after `bun run generate`.
- CI passes (lint/type-check/build).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:19:32.712577003Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:19:32.712577003Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.1","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:19:32.712577003Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-31ps.1.1","title":"Manifest web metadata: schema + types + validation","description":"## Goal
Extend manifest schema + types to include web-facing metadata needed for generated website content.

## Tasks
- Define `web` metadata fields in Module (e.g., display_name, short_desc, docs_url override, category_label, tldr_snippet, command_example, lesson_slug, visibility flags).
- Update packages/manifest/src/types.ts and schema.ts with validation rules (string lengths, optional fields).
- Update parser/validator to surface warnings for missing recommended web fields on first-class tools.
- Document defaults and migration strategy (if web fields omitted, fall back to existing content).

## Considerations
- Keep fields optional to avoid breaking existing manifest entries.
- Avoid overfitting: start with minimal set needed for current pages.
- Ensure schema constraints prevent unsafe content (no HTML injection).

## Tests
- Unit tests for schema validation (valid/invalid).
- Snapshot tests for parse + validate warnings.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schema accepts existing manifest with no changes.
- New web metadata fields validate correctly.
- Warnings emitted for missing recommended fields on critical modules.","notes":"Implemented ModuleWebMetadata: types.ts interface (20 optional fields), schema.ts Zod validation (hex color, kebab-case icon, href path regex, bounded lengths), parser.ts warnings for web-visible modules missing recommended fields, full exports in index.ts. 170 tests pass.  {"id":"bd-31ps.3","title":"Structured install logging + JSON summary artifacts","description":"## Background
Install failures are hard to diagnose without durable logs. We want deterministic artifacts: a log file and a JSON summary of phases, durations, and failures.

## Goal
Add structured install logging (log file + JSON summary) without polluting stdout.

## Scope
- Default log file under ~/.acfs/logs/install_YYYYmmdd_HHMMSS.log.
- JSON summary artifact with phase timings, failed step, and resume hints.
- Redaction for sensitive values in logs.

## Considerations
- Respect --quiet and --dry-run behaviors.
- Preserve stderr progress output.

## Testing
- Unit tests for summary JSON and redaction.
- Integration test in tests/vm to assert artifacts exist.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every install run produces a log file and summary JSON.
- Summary is stable and parseable for tooling.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:49.243580242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:49.243580242Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.3","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:49.243580242Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
#!/bin/bash
LOG=/tmp/ntm-preflight.log
echo "=== NTM Pre-flight Check: $(date) ===" | tee $LOG

# Verify tool directory exists
if [[ -d /dp/ntm ]]; then
  echo "PASS: /dp/ntm directory exists" | tee -a $LOG
else
  echo "FAIL: /dp/ntm directory NOT FOUND - aborting" | tee -a $LOG
  exit 1
fi

# Verify README exists
if [[ -f /dp/ntm/README.md ]]; then
  echo "PASS: README.md exists" | tee -a $LOG
else
  echo "WARN: README.md not found" | tee -a $LOG
fi

# Verify tool is installed and executable
if command -v ntm &>/dev/null; then
  echo "PASS: ntm command available: $(which ntm)" | tee -a $LOG
  ntm --version 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No --version flag" | tee -a $LOG
else
  echo "WARN: ntm command not in PATH" | tee -a $LOG
fi
```
